page: Microsoft Azure FX96-48ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The memory configuration is exceptionally dense, providing 1832.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 38.17 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, alongside strong performance in image and text processing. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, reaching 4135.8 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 332.13 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database and key-value store performance is highlighted by a Redis SET throughput of 74.96 million operations per second.

The hardware profile represents a high-density, memory-optimized tier. While it offers substantial CPU capabilities with advanced vector and matrix extensions, the absence of local storage means all disk operations must traverse network storage, introducing potential latency tradeoffs. The lack of a GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel training workloads, though it maintains strong CPU-based LLM prompt processing speeds. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server is positioned for workloads requiring massive memory footprints relative to compute cores, minimizing the need to scale out to larger multi-node clusters.

This configuration is suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale in-memory databases like SAP HANA or Redis, heavy data-processing pipelines, and software compilation. It also supports CPU-based machine learning inference, particularly for prompt processing where its high memory bandwidth and AMX instructions are advantageous.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96-48ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz, this server features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 1832.0 GB. It contains no local storage or GPUs, relying on network-attached Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier Geekbench single-core and multi-core performance, alongside strong memory bandwidth and high Redis throughput of 74.96 million operations per second. The server includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated vector and matrix operations. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large in-memory databases, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX96-48ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz processor, and 1832 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier Geekbench scores and is built for large in-memory databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX96-48ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 1832 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense virtual server featuring advanced matrix extensions and massive RAM capacity for demanding in-memory database workloads.

1832.0 GB of RAM providing 38.17 GB of memory per core
Top-tier Geekbench single-core and multi-core benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated vector and matrix math
No local storage, requiring network-attached Premium Storage
Best suited for large in-memory databases, Redis, and software compilation
